data.title: Consumer sentiment brightens up in May revision as inflation expectations moderate
data.description:
May University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index: 52.2 vs. prior estimate of 50.8 and 52.2 in April.

Year-ahead inflation expectations: +6.6% vs. previous estimate of +7.3% and +6.5% in April

Five-year implied inflation:+4.2% vs. initial May estimate of +4.6% and +4.4% in April.

Current Conditions Index: 58.9. vs. 57.6 prior estimate and 59.8 in April.

Expectations Index: 47.9 vs. 46.5 prior estimate and 47.3 in the previous month.

data.title: Chagee Holdings Limited GAAP EPS of $0.54, revenue of $467.5M
data.description:
• Chagee Holdings Limited press release (NASDAQ:CHA): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.54.
  • Revenue of $467.5M, increased by 35.4% Y/Y.
  • As of March 31, 2025, the company operated a total of 6,681 teahouses in Greater China and overseas, representing 63.6% growth in the number of teahouses from March 31, 2024.
  • Total GMV generated in the first quarter of 2025 was RMB8,226.8 million, representing a 38.0% increase from the same quarter of 2024, as driven by increased GMV of both Greater China and overseas teahouses.
  • Average monthly GMV per teahouse in Greater China was RMB431,973 in the first quarter of 2025, compared to RMB455,996 in the fourth quarter of 2024 and RMB549,432 in the first quarter of 2024.
  • Chagee’s Mobile Mini Program had a total of 192.4 million registered members as of March 31, 2025, representing 109.6% growth in the number of registered members from March 31, 2024.
  • Chagee had 44.9 million active members in the first quarter of 2025, representing a 21.4% year-over-year increase.

data.title: Noah Holdings Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.33, revenue of $84.7M
data.description:
• Noah Holdings press release (NYSE:NOAH): Q1 Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.33.
  • Revenue of $84.7M (-5.8% Y/Y).

  • As of March 31, 2025, the company had $561.6 million in cash and cash equivalents.

  • Net cash inflow from the company's operating activities during the first quarter of 2025 was $34.9 million.

data.title: Chicago PMI slides further into contraction territory in May
data.description:
May Chicago PMI: 40.5 vs. 45.0 consensus and 44.6 in April.

A reading under 50 indicates a contraction in the Chicago area manufacturing sector.

data.title: Bitdeer launches up to $40M stock buyback program
data.description:
Bitdeer Technologies (NASDAQ:BTDR) announced in an SEC filing on Friday the launch of an up to $40M share repurchase program.

The technology company for bitcoin mining is set to repurchase class A shares through May 29, 2026.

Bitdeer said the $20M share repurchase program approved in February is completed.

Shares were -3.21% to $13.85 during morning trade.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow pare some losses as inflation gauge comes in-line
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages pared some losses on Friday as the inflation gauge came in-line, while investors awaited any more news related to President Trump's tariffs.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.4%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.4%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) dipped 1 basis point to 4.41%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.94%.

The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit on Thursday issued an administrative stay blocking a ruling from a lower court on Wednesday that struck down “reciprocal” tariffs Donald Trump imposed in April.

So even if the court ruling is upheld, it doesn’t look like the world is heading back to the pre-2025 tariff situation, even if there is a pathway to a world with less tariffs after the ruling, Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The Fed's preferred inflation gauge rose 0.1% in April, as expected.

Wall Street's major averages on Thursday ended with marginal gains amid a bond rally sparked by weak GDP data and more back-and-forth on tariffs.

“Risk assets initially surged yesterday after the surprise court ruling, but they gave up those gains later in the day as markets digested several alternative avenues they could pursue,” Reid said.

The U.S. trade deficit narrowed in April.

The May consumer sentiment and the May Chicago PMI will land shortly. The former is expected to come in at 50.8 and the latter at 45.1.

Final May Michigan consumer sentiment data says little of itself. However, comparisons to the preliminary data suggest the evolution of partisan bias as Trump retreated from trade taxes, Donovan added.

data.title: Stripe in talks with banks about stablecoins - report
data.description:
Payment tech company Stripe (STRIP) has held early talks with banks regarding the potential use of stablecoins, one more indication that digital assets' role in global payments is gaining traction, according to a media report on Friday.

"This is not something that banks are just kind of brushing away or treating as a fad," Stripe co-founder and President John Collison told Bloomberg News. "Banks are very interested in how they should be integrated with stablecoins into their product offerings as well."

Stablecoins are designed to hold a constant value, often against a fiat currency, such as the U.S. dollar.

The company has recently introduced a range of products related to stablecoins, including a platform that allows fintechs to start their own stablecoin-linked card programs for customers.

Other payment tech companies have also ventured into the stablecoin space. In 2023, PayPayl (PYPL) launched its own stablecoin, PayPal USD (PY-USD).

Stripe (STRIP) recently closed its $1.1B acquisition of stablecoin platform Bridge.

“A lot of our future payment volume is going to be in stablecoins,” Collison told Bloomberg. “This is, for sure, a big part of our business on a go-forward basis.”

data.title: Invitation Homes estimates slowdown in renewal lease growth but higher new lease growth
data.description:
Single-family residential REIT Invitation Homes (NYSE:INVH) announced in an investor presentation on Friday an expected slowdown in the quarter-to-date renewal lease rate growth, but an increase in new lease rate growth.

Shares were +0.57% pre-market to $33.69.

As part of the company's preliminary April-May quarter-to-date same-store results, INVH delivered renewal lease rate growth of 4.7% vs. 5.2% in Q1. New lease rate growth is estimated at 2.5% vs. a decline of 0.1% in Q1.

Blended lease rate growth is expected at 4.1%, compared to 3.6% in the prior quarter.

Average occupancy is estimated at 97.3%, up from 97.2% in the previous quarter.

data.title: Zymeworks gets China's conditional approval for biliary tract cancer treatment
data.description:
• Zymeworks (NASDAQ:ZYME) said on Friday that the China health regulator has given conditional approval to zanidatamab for the treatment of metastatic HER2-positive biliary tract cancer.
  • The approval was secured by Zymeworks’ Asia Pacific partner, BeOne Medicines, under their existing license and collaboration agreement.
  • So far, Zymeworks has received $61M in upfront and milestone payments from BeOne, along with co-development funding to support zanidatamab’s clinical trials.

data.title: Canopy Growth falls after Q4 earnings miss
data.description:
Shares of Canopy Growth (NASDAQ:CGC) lost ~12% in the premarket on Friday after the Canadian cannabis player reported its Q4 results for fiscal 2025, falling well short of Street forecasts as its international operations and Storz & Bickel segment  underperformed,

The Ontario-based company reported C$65.0M in net revenue for the quarter, missing the consensus by $5.5M with ~11% YoY growth, even as Canopy’s (NASDAQ:CGC) Canadian segment offset a sharp decline in international and Storz & Bickel sales.

While Canada cannabis sales improved ~4% YoY to C$40M, backed by ~13% growth in medical cannabis, CGC’s international cannabis segment contracted ~35% YoY to generate C$8M in net revenue, and the company’s Storz & Bickel brand added C$17M, implying ~23% drop.

CGC’s adjusted gross margin fell 200 bps from the prior year period to ~19%, driving its adjusted EBITDA and GAAP loss per share to C$9M and C$1.43. Both figures missed Wall Street forecasts of $3.9M and $0.49, according to Bloomberg data, despite ~39% YoY and ~35% YoY improvement, respectively.

While the company’s free cash outflow worsened by ~60% YoY to C$36M during Q4, its full-year free cash outflow fell ~24% YoY to C$177M, mainly due to lower interest payments as the company’s total debt reached C$304M as of Mar. 31 compared to C$597M a year ago.

 “We are committed to achieving positive adjusted EBITDA in the near term and positive free cash flow over time as we accelerate growth across our global medical cannabis businesses and improve margins,” CEO Luc Mongeau remarked.

The company announced plans to improve gross margin and adj. EBITDA, driven by annualized cost savings of at least C$20M over the next 12–18 months from cost reduction opportunities identified in SG&A and Cost of Sales during Q4.
